def: prop. n. Name of a territory between Persia and Arabia (it is divided into two portions, viz. ʻirāq-ě-ʻarab, 'Arabian ʻIrāq,' the ancient Babylonia or Chaldea; and ʻirāq-ě-ʻajam, 'Persian ʻIrāq,' comprehending Media).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِراق ('irāq), from Arabic الْعِرَاق (al-ʕirāq).

def: = A عراقان ʻirāqān (nom. and obl. dual of ʻirāq), The cities of Basra and Kūfa.
= A عراقين ʻirāqain (nom. and obl. dual of ʻirāq), The cities of Basra and Kūfa.

ety: for A. اراقة, inf. n. iv of راق for روق 'to become clear,' &c.
def: s.f. Pouring out; spilling, shedding; passing urine; urine.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻirāq
def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the country called ʻIrāq; belonging to Irāq; Arabian (horse); Parthian; — an Arab horse; — a Parthian.
wety: From عراق (irāq) + ـی (-ī)

def: s.m. A fabulous garden in Arabia Felix, paradise.

ety: Pehl. erān; Zend airyana, fr. airya = S. arya, ārya
def: prop. n. Persia
adj. & s.m. Of or relating to Persia; — a Persian; a man of the Shīʻa sect.
postpos. (gov. -ke), Around, all round, round about, on every side (of), on all sides. (See gird, and cf. ās-pās).

ety: for A. عرفة, inf. n. of عرف 'to know'
def: s.m. = ʻirfān, and maʻrifat, qq.v.

ety: inf. n. iv of رفه 'to lead an easy life'
def: s.m. Bestowing affluence; comforting, cherishing; — enjoyment of ample means and tranquillity.

ety: inf. n. of عرف 'to know,' &c.
def: s.m. Knowledge, sciences, wisdom, discernment.
s.f. = ارسی irsī, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv of رجع 'to return'
def: s.m. Causing to return, returning, bringing back; changing; substituting; instituting (a suit).

ety: inf. n. iv of رقم 'to mark, stamp, &c.'
def: s.m. Act of writing: (N.B. Arabic lexicons recognize no 4th conj. of ارقام)

ety: inf. n. iv of رخو 'to become loose'
def: s.m. Slackening, loosening
subs: s.m. Letting loose the reins; unruliness.

def: s.f. = ايرکها īrkhā, q.v.
s.f. = īrshā, q.v.
s.f. Envy, jealousy; emulation, rivalry; impatience of another's success; spite, ill-will
subs: or karnā (-kī), To be envious or jealous (of); to envy; to rival
(-ko), To take ill or amiss; to feel jealous or envious.

def: s.m. Inheritance, heritage.
(v.n. of عرس; see ʻurs), s.m. & f. A spouse; a husband; a wife.

ety: inf. n. iv of رسل 'to bring a message'
def: s.m. Act of sending (as a writing, letter, &c.); despatch; remitting; remittance; monthly collections of rents forwarded to head-quarters by the subordinate revenue authorities
subs: v.t. To send, despatch, forward, remit, transmit
s.m. Invoice of goods, rents, &c. forwarded or despatched.

def: s.f. Envy, jealousy; emulation, rivalry; impatience of another's success; spite, ill-will
subs: or karnā (-kī), To be envious or jealous (of); to envy; to rival
(-ko), To take ill or amiss; to feel jealous or envious.

ety: inf. n. iv of رشد 'to take a right course'
def: s.m. Direction, instruction; order, command, bidding; will, pleasure
subs: irshād farmānā, v.t. To direct, instruct; to bid, order, command.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِرْشَاد (ʔiršād).

def: adj. & s.m. Envious, spiteful; — an envious or spiteful person.
adj. Envious, &c. = īrshālu.

ety: S. ईषा
def: s.f. Axle-tree, axle.

ety: inf. n. viii of رعش 'to tremble'
def: s.m. Trembling; tremor; trepidation.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِرْتِعَاش (irtiʕāš).

ety: inf. n. viii of ربط 'to tie, bind'
def: s.m. Connexion, affinity, familiarity, intimacy, close friendship. (See rabt̤.)

ety: inf. n. viii of رد 'to cause to return'
def: s.m. Returning, rejecting, refusing; opposing; rejection; apostasy; recantation.
wety: From Arabic اِرْتِداد (irtidād).

ety: inf. n. viii of رفع 'to elevate'
def: s.m. Elevation, exaltation; height; ascent; grandeur; prosperity.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِرْتِفَاع (irtifāʕ).

ety: inf. n. viii of رحل 'to mount, ride'
def: s.m. Act of marching or travelling; departure; death.

ety: inf. n. viii of رهن 'to deposit as a pledge'
def: s.m. Taking or receiving a thing as a pledge; pledge; hostage. (See رهن rahn.)

ety: inf. n. viii of رکب 'to ride or go upon,' 'to travel'
def: s.m. Embarking in an enterprise, venturing on an affair; perpetration or commission of a sin or crime
subs: To embark (in) an affair, venture (on) an enterprise; to perpetrate or commit a crime.
wety: From Arabic اِرْتِكَاب (irtikāb).

ety: inf. n. viii of رسم 'to mark, stamp'
def: s.m. A distinguishing mark; plan, painting, writing.

ety: inf. n. viii of رشو 'to give a bribe'
def: s.m. Taking a bribe; bribery; corruption. (See rishwat.)

ety: S. अस्य, gen. sing. neut., base इदं
def: adv. This year
subs: adv. This bank (of a river); this side
or -karnā, v.t. To put aside, put off, postpone for a long time
adv. Upon this even; for all this; still, yet, notwithstanding
adv. To this extent or degree
adv. In this case, under these circumstances; inasmuch as
adv. This instant, this moment; instantly, at once, immediately ( = is-hī dam)
adv. From this, hence; on this account, therefore, wherefore; upon this, hereupon
is t̤araḥ-se, is t̤aur, is t̤aur-se, adv. In this manner, in this wise, thus; for example
adv. To this effect; after the style or manner following
adv. With this motive or intent, with this view; to this end; to this effect
adv. To this extent or degree
is-ke kāraṇ, adv. = is wāst̤e, q.v.
adv. This moment, &c. ( = is dam); this once, for this time
adv. With this object, &c. ( = is g̠araẓ-se, q.v.)
adv. To this limit, extent or degree; in so far as; in-so-much
is mʻānī-se, adv. In this sense; to this effect; with this intent
adv. In this, herein; hereupon; meanwhile, in the meantime ( = itne-meṅ); by the way
is wāst̤e, adv. On this account; for this reason; hence, therefore
conj. For the reason that, because, for; forasmuch as
adv. Now, at present, just now; instantly, immediately, forthwith ( = isī waqt)
isī dam, isī waqt, adv. This very instant, instantly, forthwith.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀏𑀲𑁄 (eso), from Sanskrit एषः (eṣaḥ).

def: s.m. = ايش īś, q.v.
s.m. (f. īśā), Ruler, lord, master, Supreme Spirit, God; name of Śiva as regent of the north-east quarter (see īśān).
prop. n. Jesus

ety: rel. n. fr. عيسي ʻīsā
def: adj. & s.m. Christian; — a Christian
adj. & s.m. = ايشان īśān, q.v.
s.m. A name of Śiva; — the north-east

ety: inf. n. iv of اثر 'to set a mark upon'
def: s.m. Presenting, offering (a gift); an offering.
s.m. = īśwar.

ety: S. ईश्वर+मूल
def: s.m. = اسپرمول ispar-mūl, q.v.
s.f. = īśwaratā, q.v.
s.m. A poursuivant.

ety: rel. n. fr. next
def: adj. Of or belonging to Jesus; Christian
= اسپغول ispag̠ol, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اسبغول (isbaġōl).

def: = اسپند ispand, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv of ثبت
def: s.m. Establishing, confirming; confirmation, corroboration, verification, proof, demonstration; ascertainment; certain knowledge.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اثبات (isbāt), from Arabic إِثْبَات (ʔiṯbāt).

ety: inf. n. iv of صدر 'to issue or proceed' (from)
def: s.m. Producing, issuing, causing to emanate; appearing.
prop. n. = اسفہان isfahān, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِصْفَهَان (isfahān), ultimately from Old Persian *spādānaʰ (“army, adj.”) or *spādānām (“of the armies”), from 𐎿𐎱𐎠𐎭 (spādaʰ, “army”).

def: (or ispahān, i.e. sipāh+ān), prop. n. Ispahan, the capital of Persian ʻIrāq (the ancient Parthia).
s.m. Spinach.
prop. n. Name of the son of Gishtāsb, of the first dynasty of Persian kings.

ety: Gr. σπόγγος Arabicized
def: s.m. Sponge; a yeast dumpling.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْفَنْج (isfanj), borrowed from Arabic إِسْفَنْج (ʔisfanj), borrowed from Ancient Greek σπόγγος (spóngos).

def: s.f. Pole or beam of a plough; pole or shaft of a carriage.

ety: v.n. fr. عشو; see ʻashā
def: s.m. Nightfall, evening; the first watch of the night; — the prayer of sunset or nightfall ( = ʻishā-kī namāz, or namāz-ě-ʻishā).

ety: for A. اشاعة, inf. n. iv of شيع 'to be spread'
def: s.f. Spreading; spread, diffusion, propagation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشاعت (išā'at), taken from Arabic إشاعة (ʔišāʕa).

def: prop. n. Isaac.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْحَاق (ishāq), borrowed from Arabic إِسْحَاق (ʔisḥāq), borrowed from Biblical Hebrew יִצְחָק (yiṣḥāq).

ety: inf. n. iv of سہل 'to become smooth or soft'
def: s.m. Loosening, moving or producing action of (the bowels), purging; an aperient.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِسْهَال (ʔishāl).

def: s.f. Desire, wish, eagerness, inclination, choice.

ety: for A. اشارة, inf. n. iv of شور 'to show or display' (a thing)
def: v.t. To make a sign (with the hand, or head, &c., generally with the words sir-se, &c. expressed); to beckon; to nod; to wink; to nudge; to cast side glances; to point (to), to indicate; to allude (to), refer (to); to signify (a desire or wish for anything to be done), to direct by sign, &c.; to hint, give the cue (to).

ety: for A. اشارة, inf. n. iv of شور 'to show or display' (a thing)
def: v.t. To make a sign (with the hand, or head, &c., generally with the words sir-se, &c. expressed); to beckon; to nod; to wink; to nudge; to cast side glances; to point (to), to indicate; to allude (to), refer (to); to signify (a desire or wish for anything to be done), to direct by sign, &c.; to hint, give the cue (to).
adv. Little, a little, slightly, somewhat.

ety: inf. n. iv of شفق
def: s.m. Tenderness, compassion ( = shafaqat).
wety: From Classical Persian اشفاق (ašfāq, išfāq), from Arabic إِشْفَاق (ʔišfāq), verbal noun of أَشْفَقَ (ʔašfaqa), and أَشْفَاق (ʔašfāq), the plural of شَفَق (šafaq).

ety: inf. n. iv of شہد 'to give evidence'
def: s.m. Taking to witness; adducing as evidence, bringing proof.

ety: inf. n. of عشق 'to love passionately'
def: s.m. Love, excessive love, passion (for, -kā)
subs: 'Love of God,' a form of salutation used (instead of salām) by faqīrs, and by wrestlers when they meet in the arena
adj. & s.m. Amorous; — a gallant; a rake
s.f. Amorous toying, love-making, gallantry, amour
s.m. A species of ivy that bears red flowers; — American jasmine, Ipomæa quamoclit, or Q. vulgaris
adj. & s.m. Amorous; — one practised in love, a lover
An exclamation of praise; excellent! well done! bravo!
ex: تیرے عشق میں ہم نے کیا کیا نہ دیکھا؟
tere iśq mẽ ham ne kyā kyā na dekhā?
What did I not see in your love?
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِشْق (išq), borrowed from Arabic عِشْق (ʕišq). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi عشق ('śq /⁠'iśq⁠/).

ety: inf. n. iv of شکل 'to become dubious'
def: Ambiguity, difficulty, suspicion, painfulness.
s.m. One who excites doubts or difficulties.

ety: from ishkāl
def: v.t. To excite suspicion; to deceive, tempt, delude, to wheedle.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻishq
def: adj. & s.m. Of or relating to love; amatory, erotic; — an amorous man; a lover.

ety: inf. n. iv of شمّ 'to smell'
def: s.m. Diffusing odour, shedding perfume; (Gram.) giving to a quiescent consonant a slight sound or smack of ẓamma or kasra, but so as not to lengthen the syllable or make an additionable syllable, as in ḵẖẉesh.

ety: inf. n. iv of شرق 'to rise, shine' (the sun, &c.)
def: s.m. Rising (of the sun), sun-rise, morning; splendour, lustre, beauty.

ety: inf. n. iv of شرك 'to share'
def: s.m. Participating; entering into partnership.
adj. Of or pertaining to sunrise, morning; Eastern, oriental; having the splendour of the East

ety: for A. عشرة, fr. muʻāsharat, 'holding social or familiar intercourse,' inf. n. iii of عشر 'to make ten of'
def: s.f. Social or familiar intercourse, pleasant and familiar conversation, society; pleasure, enjoyment, mirth
subs: s.m., or ʻishrat-kada, s.m., or ʻishrat-gāh, s.f. A house of pleasure, a place of entertainment
adj. & s.m. Pleasure-increasing; — one who increases enjoyment or mirth
v.n. To enjoy oneself, to take enjoyment or pleasure, to revel.

def: part. adj. Desired, wished for; approved; cherished, beloved; reverenced, worshipped; — s.m. An object of desire, love or reverence; a god, deity; a beloved person; one chosen or favoured of a god or goddess; faith, trust, love
s.m. & adj. Stamp. (See اسٿام isṭām.)
s.f. Desirableness; belovedness; respect, reverence.

ety: superl. of ishṭa
def: adj. Most desired, best beloved, dearest.

ety: compar. of ishṭa
def: adj. More desired, dearer, more loved.

ety: inf. n. viii of شعل 'to burn'
def: s.m. Burning, blazing; inflaming; lighting a fire or candle; — fomenting, instigating a quarrel.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic اِشْتِعَال (ištiʕāl).

ety: i.e. ishtěʻāl+dim. aff. ak
def: s.f. A small flame; a quarrel
subs: v.t. To foment (quarrels); to incite to bad actions; to abet.

ety: inf. n. viii of شہو 'To be pleasant'
def: s.f. Desire, appetite, longing; hunger.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اشتها (ištihā), from Arabic اِشْتِهَاء (ištihāʔ).

ety: inf. n. viii of شہر 'to make public'
def: s.m. Publication, divulging; publicity, public notice; notification, announcement, advertisement, proclamation, declaration, notice, placard, poster; fame, rumour, report, renown
subs: (-ko), To give publicity (to), to notify, announce; to promulgate; to give notice; to publish, proclaim
v.t. To publish, proclaim; to divulge
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِهَار (ištihār).

def: s.m. Lit. 'One who is advertised'; a person who has absconded.
s.f. Sacrificing, sacrifice; sacrificial pile.

ety: inf. n. viii of شبه 'to assimilate,' &c.
def: s.m. Doubt ( = shubha), suspicion; ambiguity; scruple; — likening; comparison.
adj. Doubtful, suspicious; ambiguous.

ety: inf. n. viii of شد 'to make hard, firm,' &c.
def: s.m. Confirming, strengthening, increasing in violence.

ety: inf. n. viii of شغل 'to busy, to occupy'
def: s.m. Occupation, employment ( = shug̠l), business, study.

ety: inf. n. viii of شق 'to cleave, split'
def: s.m. Derivation, etymology.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِشْتِقَاق (ištiqāq).

ety: inf. n. viii of شمل 'to include'
def: s.m. Comprehension, containing.

ety: inf. n. viii of شري 'to buy; to sell'
def: s.m. Buying; selling; commerce.

ety: inf. n. viii of شرك 'to share'
def: s.m. Partnership, fellowship ( = shirkat and sājhā), participation, communion; — (in lexicology) the being homonymous; homonymy.

ety: inf. n. viii of شوق 'to excite desire'
def: s.m. Desire, strong inclination, longing, craving, yearning; love, fondness
subs: (-kā), To be eagerly desirous (of); to long (for); to yearn (after); &c.

def: s.f. = S ايشتو ईशित्व īśitwa, s.m. Superiority, supremacy, lordship; one of the eight attributes of the Deity.
s.m. = S ايشتا ईशिता īśitā, s.f. Superiority, supremacy, lordship; one of the eight attributes of the Deity.
prop. n. Alexander.
adj. Of or relating to Alexander.
prop. n. Alexandria (in Egypt).

ety: S. स्कन्ध
def: s.m. Section (of a book), book, chapter.

ety: inf. n. iv of سقط 'to fall, drop'
def: s.m. Causing to fall; dropping or casting her young (an animal); miscarrying, miscarriage; procuring an abortion
subs: s.m. Miscarriage; procuring an abortion; abortion.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِسْقَاط (ʔisqāṭ).

ety: inf. n. iv of سکت 'to become still or quiet'
def: s.f. Silencing; pacifying, soothing, quieting, assuaging, allaying.

ety: Gr. σχίλλα Arabicized
def: s.m. A squill, a wild onion.

ety: inf. n. iv of سلو 'to experience comfort'
def: s.m. Comfort, consolation.

ety: inf. n. iv of صلح 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.f. Correction, improvement, amendment, emendation, adjustment; revision; — dressing or trimming the beard; the hair on the chin
subs: To trim or dress the beard; to shave the chin
adj. Capable of correction or remedy, remediable
To be remediable; to be or become corrected
v.t. To correct, amend, improve, rectify, reform, cure; to correct or improve penmanship or composition
(-se), To be corrected (by); to have (a thing, especially writing or composition) corrected (by).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِصْلَاح (ʔiṣlāḥ).

ety: inf. n. iv of سلم 'to be or become safe'
def: s.m. The Mohammadan religion; orthodoxy (according to Moḥammadans)
subs: islām qubūl karnā, v.n. To profess the Moḥammadan religion; to be converted to Moḥammadanism.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْلَام (islām), borrowed from Arabic إِسْلَام (ʔislām), from أَسْلَمَ (ʔaslama).

def: adj. Faithful; orthodox (according to Moḥammadans); — s.m. A follower of the Moḥammadan religion, a Muslim.
wety: From اسلام (islām, “Islam”) + ـی (-ī)

def: conj. v.s.v. اس is.

ety: rt. سمو
def: s.m. Name, appellation; (in Gram.) a noun
subs: Lit. 'The great name'; the name of the Almighty; one of the ninety-nine names or attributes of God, the utterance of which is an irresistible spell over evil spirits
A name fitly denoting the qualities of the named
An aplastic noun, a primitive noun
A name importing something good or beautiful
The present participle; the noun indicating the state or condition of the subject
s.f. List, roll or register of names, muster-roll; the act of enrolling; registry-office
s.f. List of witnesses; petition to summon witnesses
adj. & adv. Entry (in statements) according to the order of individual names.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْم (ism), borrowed from Arabic اِسْم (ism).

def: prop. n. Ishmael, son of Abraham.

ety: for A. عصمة, inf. or v.n. of عصم 'to defend,' &c.
def: s.f. Defence, protection, preservation (esp. fr. sin); — honour, integrity; continence, chastity; the parda or seclusion in which (oriental) women are required to live.
adj. Nominal (as opposed to verbal); by name, named.
adj. f. = اسمی ismī, q.v. (e.g. jumlaʼi-ismīya, a nominal sentence).

ety: S. स्मृति
def: s.f. The Hindū law. (See smr̤iti.)
v.s.v. اسم ism.
adj. (f. -a), Twelve; the twelve Imāms.
adj. Relating to the twelve Imāms.

ety: inf. n. iv of سند 'to lean' (upon)
def: s.m. Resting a thing upon (another); alleging on the authority of another.

s.m. Herb of grace; seed of wild rue; seed of the meṅhdī or Lawsonia inermis; a species of mustard seed (the seed is burnt at marriages and births to drive away evil spirits, or to avert the mischievous effects of evil eyes: — also written sipand); syn. kālā dānā).

ety: S. ईश्वर+मूल
def: s.m. The plant Indian birthwort, Aristolochia Indica. (It is said to be a valuable antidote to snake-bites, being applied both externally and internally

ety: Port. espada
def: s.m. Steel (syn. faulād).
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese espada, from Latin spatha, from Ancient Greek σπάθη (spáthē).

ety: S. स्पृहा, the a being red. in Hindī
def: s.f. Desire, wish, inclination.

ety: inf. n. iv of سرف 'to be negligent,' &c.
def: s.m. Excess, extravagance, profusion, prodigality, lavish expenditure, abuse of wealth; dissipation, ruin.
wety: From Arabic إِسْرَاف (ʔisrāf).

ety: inf. n. iv of صرف 'to turn'; to exchange; to expend'
def: s.m. Expenditure; waste, extravagance, prodigality
subs: v.t. To expend; to squander, waste, dissipate, &c.

def: s.m. The name of an angel who, it is said, will sound the last trumpet at the resurrection; a seraph.
prop. n. Israel.

ety: inf. n. iv of سرّ 'to gladden'
def: s.m. Keeping secret; concealment, suppression; secrecy, secret, mystery; influence of an evil spirit
wety: From Arabic أَسْرَار (ʔasrār), plural of سِرّ (sirr).

ety: inf. n. iv of صرّ 'to make a prolonged sound,' &c.
def: s.m. Persevering; persisting (in); perseverance; obstinacy.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِصْرَار (ʔiṣrār).

def: s.m. Minium, cinnabar.
s.m. = ايشور īśwar, q.v.

ety: Bar. Gr. σταβλιον; Lat. stabulum
def: s.m. A stable.
s.m. A stable (see اسطبل ist̤abal).
ex: گھوڑے کو اصطبل میں لےکر کھانا دو۔
ghoṛe ko astabal mẽ lekar khānā do.
Take the horse to the stable and give it some food.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اصطبل (istabl), from Arabic إِصْطَبْل (ʔiṣṭabl), via Byzantine Greek στάβλον (stáblon) from Latin stabulum.

ety: P. ستبره sitabra, Arabicised
def: s.m. Thick silk brocade interwoven with gold; silk or satin (particularly green) the colour of which seems to change agreeably to the light in which it is seen.

ety: v.n. of istādan, 'to stand' = Pehl. ostātan, from Z. stā = S. स्था
def: part. adj. = استاده istāda, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اُسْتَاد (ustād). Doublet of اُسْتاذ (ustāz).

ety: perf. part. of īstādan; Pehl. ośṭātan, rt. Zend. stā = S. sthā
def: part. adj. Erected, set up; standing
subs: v.t. To raise up, to erect, &c.

ety: perf. part. of istādan, 'to stand'; see istād
def: part. adj. Erected, set up; standing; erect; — s.m. Pole, ensign-staff; stay, support, prop (especially) a prop for supporting the door of a tent, or a canopy.

ety: abst. subst. from next
def: s.f. Erection; stability.

ety: abst. subst. from istāda, q.v.
def: s.f. Standing, stand, stop, stay, erection; firmness, resistance.
s.m. (corr. of the English), Stamp; postage-stamp (syn. ṭikaṭ)
(ειστηνπολιν), prop. n. The city of Constantinople (also written استانبول istāmbol).
s.m. (corr. of the English), Stamp; postage-stamp (syn. ṭikaṭ)
s.m. (corr. of the English), Assistant.
s.m. A weight of four and a half mis̤qāls or six and a half drachms.

ety: στύραξ Arabicised
def: s.m. Storax.

ety: S. स्तृ 'spreading out'
def: s.f. Smoothing-iron; a tailor's goose
subs: v.t. To smooth with a heated iron, to iron
s.m. A person who irons linen.

ety: inf. n. x of عون; iv. اعان 'to help'
def: s.f. Seeking assistance, praying for help (ʻaun); aid, help, assistance ( = ʻaun); protection.

ety: inf. n. x of عور; iv. اعار 'to lend'
def: s.m. Asking a loan (ʻārīyat), borrowing; using a word metaphorically; metaphor, figure

ety: inf. n. x of عدّ; iv. اعدّ 'to make ready,' &c.
def: s.f. Getting oneself ready; readiness, preparation; proficiency; ability, means; capability, capacity, mental power, talent, parts; aptitude, dexterity.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِعْدَاد (istiʕdād).

ety: inf. n. x of عفو 'to efface'
def: s.m. Seeking to be excused (from); asking forgiveness, deprecating; resignation (of an office or appointment)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِعْفَا (isti'fā), borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِعْفَاء (istiʕfāʔ).

ety: inf. n. x of حول 'to be altered or changed'
def: s.m. Change of state, undergoing a change; becoming, or deeming, absurd, inconsistent, unreal or impossible.

ety: inf. n. x of حيض 'to menstruate'
def: s.m. Immoderate flux of the menses; menstrual discharge at an undue period or time.

ety: inf. n. x of حبّ 'to be loved'
def: s.m. Contracting friendship; — an action the performance of which is meritorious and the omission not criminal; work of supererogation.

ety: inf. n. x of حق 'to be or become just, right,' &c.
def: s.m. Demanding as a right or due, demanding justice; claim, demand, right, due, title; merit; ability
subs: Equity of redemption of a mortgage
Right of preëmption, preëmptive title ( = ḥaqq-i-shufʻa)
Right of way; exercise or use of a right

ety: inf. n. x of حکم 'to restrain,' &c.
def: s.m. Firmness, strength; confirmation, corroboration, ratification; fixity
subs: (-ko), To strengthen, make firm; to confirm; to corroborate; to ratify, sanction, make valid or legal; to verify, attest.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِحْکَام (istihkām), from Arabic اِسْتِحْكَام (istiḥkām).

ety: inf. n. x of حقر 'to be contemptible,' &c.
def: s.m. Treating with contempt, despising, scorning, vilifying.

ety: inf. n. x of حلف 'to swear'
def: s.m. Causing to swear; exacting an oath.

ety: inf. n. x of هلك 'to destroy'
def: s.m. Seeking the death or ruin of any one; — ruining, destroying, consuming ( = halāk).

ety: inf. n. x of هلّ 'to appear' (the new moon)
def: s.m. The appearing of the new moon (hilāl).

ety: inf. n. x of حسن 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.m. Approving, praising; taking or regarding as a favour.

ety: inf. n. x of هزأ 'to laugh at,' &c.
def: s.m. Laughing at; derision, scorn, scoff; jest, joke.
wety: From Arabic اِسْتِهْزَاء (istihzāʔ).

ety: inf. n. x of حضر 'to become present'
def: s.m. Calling, citing to appear, summoning before.

ety: inf. n. x of عجل 'to make haste'
def: s.m. Wishing to make haste; requiring haste or expedition (from a person, or in a matter); hastening, accelerating.

ety: inf. n. x of علو 'to be high'
def: s.m. Superiority; exaltation; seeking promotion.

ety: inf. n. x of عمل 'to work, serve'
def: s.m. Work, operation; employment, use; practice, exercise; custom, usage, observance; fashion
subs: v.n. To be in use, be current (a word, or phrase)
(-kā), To make use (of); to use, employ, put in use, apply
v.n. To come into use, to be in use, be used
(-ko), To bring into use, put in use or in operation, put in practice; to apply; to use, employ ( = istěʻmāl karnā); to utilize
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian استعمال (isti'māl), from Arabic اِسْتِعْمَال (istiʕmāl).

def: adj. Used; worn, second-hand; usual, customary; fit for use; practical: — s.m. The best kind of rice.
s.m. (corr. of the English), Railway station; police station.

ety: S. स्थापित
def: s.m. Setting up or placing an idol in a temple.

ety: S. स्थिर
def: adj. Standing, stagnant; stable, steady, fixed, firm, settled, immovable.
s.f. = ستهرتا स्थिरता, sthiratā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. x of وعب 'to take the whole,' &c.
def: s.m. Taking the whole; extirpating; asking in a present.

ety: inf. n. viii of صبغ 'to dye; to dip
def: s.m. Dyeing, staining; dipping, immersion, baptism ( = baptizma).

ety: inf. n. x of بدّ 'to remove or withdraw with'
def: s.m. Absolute dominion, despotism; insisting upon a thing being done.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِبْدَاد (istibdād), borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِبْدَاد (istibdād), from اِسْتَبَدَّ (istabadda).

ety: inf. n. x of برأ 'to become clear or free of'
def: s.m. Cleansing; washing one's hands (of a business); desiring to be freed (from a business); the waiting of a woman for a certain time (three months according to Mohammadan law) after being divorced from her husband, to ascertain whether she is pregnant or not, before she can contract another marriage.

ety: inf. n. x of دعو 'to seek, pray for,' &c.
def: s.f. Asking, supplicating; desire; request, demand; claim; supplication, prayer, humble petition.

ety: inf. n. x of دوم 'to continue or last'
def: s.f. Seeking or desiring permanence; wishing to be quiet, steady, &c.; acting with deliberation; assiduity.

ety: inf. n. x of دين 'to borrow'
def: s.f. Contracting a debt, borrowing; demanding payment of a debt, suing for a debt; limited liability (of a partner in business).

ety: inf. n. x of دلّ 'to direct aright'
def: s.m. Seeking proof, arguments or reasons; proof, demonstration, argument ( = دليل dalīl, q.v.).
wety: Ultimately from Arabic اِسْتِدْلَال (istidlāl).

ety: inf. n. x of درج 'to go step by step'
def: s.m. Withdrawing or advancing gradually; seeking to remove by degrees.

ety: inf. n. x of درك 'to reach'
def: s.m. Reaching, overtaking, obtaining; comprehending, understanding; (in Gram.) emendation; contrariety, antithesis (e.g. ḥarf-i-istidrāk, particle of emendation, adversative particle).

ety: inf. n. x of وفی 'to be full,' &c.
def: s.m. Satisfying completely; paying or receiving in full of all demands; renouncing, relinquishing, resigning (an appointment, see istěʻfā).

ety: inf. n. x of فيد; iv افاد 'to derive advantage'
def: s.m. Seeking gain or advantage (fāʼida); attainment; profit, gain, advantage ( = fāʼida).

ety: inf. n. x of فہم 'to understand'
def: s.m. Desiring or seeking information, asking for an explanation; inquiry, interrogation.
wety: From Arabic اِسْتِفْهَام (istifhām).

def: adj. Interrogatory; interrogative (particle or pronoun).

ety: inf. n. x of فرغ 'to pour forth,' &c.
def: s.m. Vomiting; rejecting; belching.

ety: inf. n. x of فسر 'to declare, explain,' &c.
def: s.m. Seeking information (of); inquiry, question, interrogation; investigation, search; reference
subs: (-se), To inquire (of), require an explanation (from); to question, interrogate; to call for information; to make a reference (to).
wety: From Arabic اِسْتِفْسَار (istifsār).

def: s.f. Statement of a prosecutor subject to investigation.

ety: inf. n. x of فتو
def: s.m. Taking a legal opinion (fatwa), consulting a lawyer or doctor of law.

ety: inf. n. x of غوث 'to cry for help'
def: s.m. Asking for help; demanding justice; moving (a court) for justice; complaining; complaint; suit
subs: To demand justice; to seek redress; to sue.

ety: inf. n. x of غفر 'to pardon'
def: s.m. Asking pardon, begging mercy; craving grace; deprecating; deprecation; the (Mohamadan's) prayer for defence against the evil spirit (Shait̤ān); mercy; deliverance
subs: To seek pardon or forgiveness of God; to crave mercy; to deprecate.

ety: inf. x of غني 'to be able to dispense with'
def: s.m. Ability to dispense with, independence (in point of fortune), opulence; content; — adj. Independent; contented.

ety: inf. n. x of غرب 'to be obscure or unintelligible'
def: s.m. Great admiration; amazement, wonder.

ety: inf. n. x of غرق 'to be submerged, to sink'
def: s.m. Drowning; the being immersed, absorbed, or wholly engaged (in anything); immersion; lien; mortgage
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِغْرَاق (istiḡrāq).

ety: inf. n. x of جوب 'to rend,' &c.
def: s.f. Answering, accepting; hearing or answering prayer, receiving a petition.

ety: inf. n. x of جوز 'to be allowable'
def: s.f. Asking leave, begging permission.

ety: inf. n. x of قوم 'to stand, rise up'
def: s.f. Keeping oneself upright, standing erect; standing still; rectitude, integrity; stability; durability; firmness, constancy.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِسْتِقَامَة (istiqāma).

ety: inf. n. x of قبل 'to come forward'
def: s.m. Encountering, meeting; the ceremony of meeting and receiving a visitor; reception, welcome; futurity; (in Gram.) the future tense
subs: (-kā), To advance to meet (a person); to receive or welcome (a person); to go forth to meet and welcome (a guest).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian استقبال (istiqbāl), from Arabic اِسْتِقْبَال (istiqbāl).

ety: inf. n. x of کبر 'to be great or large'
def: s.m. Pride, conceit, presumption.

ety: Old P. istakhar, Zend śtakhra, 'firm,' rt. śtak = S. स्तक्
def: s.m. 'Standing firm'; the ancient Persepolis.

ety: inf. n. x of خير 'to be possessed of good'
def: s.m. Seeking to obtain the Divine favour; looking into the Qoʼrān, or the Bible, for a good augury or omen; judging from omens or augury; stichomancy; bibliomancy.

ety: inf. n. x of خدم 'to serve'
def: s.m. Employing, making use of the labour of another; seeking or getting employment.

ety: inf. n. x of خفّ 'to be light'
def: s.m. Holding in slight esteem, making light of, despising; indignity.

ety: inf. n. x of خلص 'to be clear'
def: s.m. Seeking to liberate; liberation, release; liberty, freedom.

ety: inf. n. x of خرج 'to go out or forth'
def: s.m. Causing to go or come out, expulsion, banishment; rejection; taking out, drawing forth, extraction.

ety: inf. n. x of قلّ 'to lift, raise up'
def: s.m. Absolute power or authority, sovereignty, supremacy; independence; absoluteness; intrepidity; firmness, resolution, constancy; steadiness; perseverance, persistence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِقْلَال (istiqlāl), from Arabic اِسْتِقْلَال (istiqlāl).

def: adj. ( = mustaqil), Having absolute power; invested with authority; confirming in possession

ety: inf. n. x of کمل 'to be perfect'
def: s.m. Seeking the completion or perfection (of any thing); completion; bringing to perfection.

ety: inf. n. x of قراً 'to read'
def: s.m. Reasoning from induction.

ety: inf. n. x of کره 'to dislike'
def: s.m. Aversion, loathing, abhorrence; reluctance.

ety: inf. n. x of قرّ 'to remain firm, rest, &c.'
def: s.m. Requiring or calling for settlement or affirmation; confirming, ratifying; confirmation, ratification; declaration; recognition.

ety: inf. n. x of کشف 'to open, make manifest'
def: s.m. Desiring or requesting the manifestation (of anything); exposition or laying open (of anything).

ety: inf. n. x of ولي 'to be master of, control'
def: s.m. Predominance, conquest; domination, ascendency, authority.

ety: inf. n. viii of صلح 'to be good, suitable, agreeable,' &c.
def: s.f. Phrase, phraseology, idiom, usage; accepted signification, conventional meaning; conventional term, technical term.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِصْطِلَاح (istilāh), from Arabic اِصْطِلَاح (iṣṭilāḥ).

def: s.f. pl. (of iṣt̤ilāḥ), Phrases, idioms; conventional terms, technicalities.
adj. Phraseological, idiomatic; conventional, technical
subs: s.f. The conventional or secondary meaning.

ety: inf. n. x of لزم 'to be necessary'
def: s.m. Rendering necessary; requiring; necessity.

ety: inf. n. viii of سمع 'to hear'
def: s.m. Hearing, listening (to music, particularly); — what is heard; tidings, report, rumour.

ety: inf. n. x of ميل 'to incline to
def: s.f. Caressing, conciliating; soothing, consoling; winning or gaining over; solace; encouragement.

ety: inf. n. x of نبط 'to draw (from)'
def: s.m. Drawing out, extraction; deduction
subs: (-se), To draw forth, extract; to elicit; to deduce; to select.

ety: inf. n. x of مدّ 'to extend,' &c.
def: s.m. Desiring or begging assistance (madad), craving help; asking for supplies or subsidies; application for help or protection.

ety: inf. n. x of مرّ 'to pass through'; 'to continue'
def: s.m. Continuation, continuance, perseverance; perpetuity, permanence; uninterrupted tenure of possession; (in law), fixed rent, not liable to alteration
subs: Holder of a farm or lease in perpetuity.

def: adj. Continuative, perpetual, permanent, unceasing; — s.f. Land rented on a fixed lease; land permanently settled; the permanent settlement (of Lord Cornwallis)
subs: s.m. Lease or farm granted at a fixed rent; lease in perpetuity
s.f. Fixed or perpetual assessment of rent

ety: inf. n. x of متع 'to enjoy'
def: s.m. Enjoying, reaping the fruits (of); being delighted; celebrating the solemnities of the festival of Mecca; — enjoyment (of a woman).

ety: inf. n. x of مزج
def: s.m. Seeking to discover one's purpose or wish (mizāj), sounding one's intention; asking an opinion.

ety: inf. viii of سند 'to lean (upon)'
def: s.m. Leaning or resting (upon).

ety: inf. n. x of انس 'to be or become familiar'
def: s.m. Familiarity, intimacy, sympathy.

ety: inf. n. x of نجو 'to pass out of the body' (urine, &c.)
def: s.m. Ablution or purification after the offices of nature ( = āb-dast); making water; the private parts; — istinje-se istinjā laṛanā, To have carnal intercourse with
subs: s.m. A lump of earth or clay (such as is used for purification when water is not obtainable; hence), anything worthless
(-ke), To grow fast friends, become intimate.
wety: From Classical Persian استنجا (istinjā), from Arabic اِسْتِنْجَاء (istinjāʔ).

ety: inf. n. x of نشق 'to snuff up'
def: s.m. Throwing water up the nostrils when performing the waẓū; snuffing up the air, or odours, &c.

ety: inf. n. x of روح 'to rest'
def: s.f. Ease, rest, repose, tranquillity; sleep.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِسْتِرَاحَت (istirāhat), from Arabic اِسْتِرَاحَة (istirāḥa).

ety: inf. n. x of ردّ 'to return, reject'
def: s.m. Demanding restitution of anything, whether given, deposited in trust, or taken without consent; — revoking, recalling, retracting; reversing, repealing; rejecting; repelling, driving or forcing back
subs: v.t. To revoke, retract, repeal, annul, reverse, set aside
s.m. Reversal of a public sale.

ety: inf. n. x of رجع 'to return'
def: s.m. Returning (to God); having recourse to God during affliction or misfortune; repeating the words (of the Qorʼān, ii. 151) "Verily to God we belong, and verily unto Him we return."

ety: inf. n. x of رضو 'to be pleased'
def: s.f. Seeking to please; asking or seeking the consent; willingness, alacrity; pleasure; assent, consent ( = raẓā-mandī).

ety: inf. n. x of سعد 'to be happy'
def: s.m. Desiring happiness; being fortunate; deeming or reckoning fortunate, auspicious or lucky; conceiving well of anything.

ety: inf. n. x of اصل 'to have root or foundation'
def: s.m. Rooting up, extirpating, eradicating, destroying, exterminating.

ety: inf. n. x of شہد 'to bear witness to'
def: s.m. Summoning witnesses; bringing testimony or proof; taking evidence; falling a martyr to religion.

ety: inf. n. x of سقی 'to give to drink'
def: s.m. Dropsy ( = jalandar)
subs: Dropsy of the whole body, Anasarca

ety: inf. n. x of ثقل 'to be heavy'
def: s.m. Heaviness; trouble; importunity.

ety: inf. n. x of ثنی 'to double or fold'
def: s.m. Exception, exclusion, setting aside, distinction; rejection.

ety: inf. n. x of 'to make or construct'
def: s.m. Requisition of workmanship, forced labour; pressing into service.
adj. Exceptional.

ety: inf. n. x of صوب 'to pour forth'
def: s.m. Deeming right; approving, approbation; consulting as to what is right or proper to be done; consultation, reference; inquiry; taking the opinion of
subs: v.t. To take the opinion (of), inquire (of); to refer (to); to consult.

ety: inf. n. x of طوع 'to be submis sive'
def: s.f. Power, ability; possibility; compliance with command, submission, obedience; dependence.

ety: inf. n. viii of ستر 'to hide'
def: s.m. Act of concealing; concealment.

ety: inf. n. viii of سوي 'to be equal'
def: s.m. Equality; parallelism; what is plane, level or parallel; (the word is ordinarily met in the phrase) ḵẖat̤t̤i-istiwā, The equinoctial line, the equator.

ety: inf. n. x of ظہر 'to be apparent,' &c.
def: s.m. Seeking assistance; help, support; power; calling to mind, remembering; repeating by heart; reciting.

ety: S. स्त्री
def: s.f. Woman; wife
subs: s.m. Settlement made on a wife by her father; jointure; wife's property or paraphernalia
v.t. To take a wife, to marry
s.m. One who goes after women, an adulterer

def: (pl. of اسطقس = Gr. στοιχεῖον), Elements, principles
s.m. = īśwar, q.v.
s.m. Ruler, master, lord; the Supreme Being, God; — a name of Śiva, of Kām-deʼo, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Subject to or dependent on God; — one who is resigned to the Divine will, a servant of God
s.f. Subjection to, or dependence on, God
s.f. The worship of God ( = pūjā)
(˚ra+aṅ˚), s.m. lit. 'A part of God'; an epithet of created things, especially animals
(īśvara+vānī), s.f. The voice of God, revelation, inspiration, oracle
s.m. A worshipper of God, a pious person
s.m. Remembering God, calling God to mind
s.m. Any place of divine worship, temple, mosque, church, synagogue.

def: s.f. Supremacy, lordship; divineness of nature, divinity, Godhead; divine or Almighty power; the divine faculties of omnipresence, omnipotence, invisibility, &c.
s.f. Mistress, queen, goddess; name of Durgā; of Lakshmī, or of any other of the Śaktis or female energies of the deities.
adj. Pertaining to deity; divine, godlike.
s.f. = aiśwarya, q.v.
s.f. Supremacy, lordship; divineness of nature, divinity, Godhead; divine or Almighty power; the divine faculties of omnipresence, omnipotence, invisibility, &c.
v.s.v. اس is.

ety: inf. n. of عصی 'to disobey, to rebel,' &c.
def: s.m. Disobedience, rebellion; sin, transgression.
s.f. = īṅṭ, q.v.

ety: S. इतद्
def: adv. Now, at this time, at present.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَتَا (atā), from a Turkic language.

ety: S. इतस् or इत्र
def: adv. Here, hither
subs: adv. Here and there, hither and thither; this side and that, to and fro.

ety: inf. n. iv of طوع 'to be or become submissive'
def: s.f. Obedience, submission, subjection, subordination, fealty, allegiance; observance; reverence, worship, homage; obsequiousness
subs: (-kī), To obey, do the bidding (of); to submit (to); to observe; to comply (with); to serve, pay homage (to); to worship.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِطَاعَت (itā'at), borrowed from Arabic إِطَاعَة (ʔiṭāʕa), from أَطَاعَ (ʔaṭāʕa). First attested in c. 1500 as Middle Hindi اِطاعَت (at̤a't /⁠itā'at⁠/).

ety: S. इयत्+क
def: adv. So much, this much.

ety: S. एतद्+ काल
def: adv. At this time, now, at present ( = اتا itā).
adv. Now, &c. = itāl, q.v.

ety: S. तॄ?
def: part. adj. Apprized, cautioned, warned; on one's guard, &c. The word is only met with in the phrase اترجانا itar jānā, To be apprized, &c.; to suspect. (Cf. تاڙجانا tāṛ jānā.)

ety: v.n. of اترانا itrānā, q.v.
def: adj. Walking or acting affectedly; strutting; affected; playful, wanton, coquettish; giving oneself airs, pompous, ostentatious, consequential, conceited, proud, haughty, arrogant

ety: see itrānā
def: adj. & s.m. Acting affectedly, affected, apish; — affected person, swaggerer, swell.
(Braj) = اتا ittā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv. of تبع
def: s.m. Inducing to follow following, obeying; obedience.
(Braj) = (1) ات ita, and, (2), اتنے itne, qq.v.
(Prov.) = اتنا itnā, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv of طفأ 'to become extinguished' (fire)
def: s.m. Extinction or quenching (of fire).
adj. Desired, beloved, &c. = ishṭa, q.v.

ety: inf. n. iv of طہر 'to become pure'
def: s.m. Purifying; purification.

ety: S. इषिता, rt. इष्
def: s.f. Spear, lance; spear staff; the cat in the boys' game of 'cat and trap'
subs: (ḍandā), The ball and crook in the game of ćaugān.

ety: S. आ+वेष्ट् or वृत्
def: v.n. To walk affectedly or coquettishly; to express tenderness or kindness by gestures; — to speak affectedly, to drawl; to roll or mouth one's words; to mumble; to stammer; to lisp; to speak indistinctly; — to evade a direct answer (to a question or remark); to give a pert answer; to speak snappishly, crossly or rudely; to affect ignorance.
adv. This much, thus much, so much; — (at the end of a book, chapter, letter, &c.) finis.
= ات itī, q.v.
s.f. Any calamity of the season (as drought, excessive rain, noxious insects, &c.); swarm of gnats, mosquitoes, &c.; seasons? ( = ṛtod;itu).

ety: S. इषिता, rt. इष्
def: s.f. Spear, lance; spear staff; the cat in the boys' game of 'cat and trap'
subs: (ḍandā), The ball and crook in the game of ćaugān.

def: = اتہاس itihās, q.v.
s.m. Story, fable, narrative, annals, history; tradition
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit इतिहास (itihāsa).

ety: inf. n. viii of الف 'to become sociable,' &c.
def: s.m. Familiarity, friendship, society, company, connection, correspondence.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِيتِلَاف (ītilāf).

ety: inf. n. iv of طلق 'to become loosed'
def: s.m. Loosing, setting at liberty; divorcing, repudiating; disengagedness, freedom; application, reference; rendering absolute or universal
subs: (-se), To be separate (from), to be independent (of); to correspond (with), to be applicable (to); — it̤lāq karnā (-par), To apply (to); to use (a word or phrase in a certain sense).

ety: inf. n. iv of طمع 'to covet'
def: s.m. Strong desire, longing, covetousness.

ety: inf. n. iv of تم
def: s.m. Perfection, completion, accomplishment
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اتمام (itmām), from Arabic إِتْمَام (ʔitmām).

ety: inf. n. iv of طمأن 'to be or become at rest'
def: s.m. Quiet, rest, repose, calm, peace, tranquillity, composure; content, satisfaction; security; assurance, confidence, reliance, trust; surety, guarantee
subs: v.t. To trust (in), rely (on), feel assured (of); to believe (in), to credit
(-ko), To feel reassured, feel at ease; to be satisfied.

ety: S. इयत्ता, Pr. एत्तिणा
def: adj. (f. -ī), As much as this, this much; so much, so many
subs: s.m. Small matter; small quantity; trifle
adv. By this time; all this time
adv. In the meantime, meanwhile; at this moment; upon this.

def: (Braj.) = اتنا itnā, q.v.
s.m. Perfume, fragrance; essence; ottar (or otto) of roses, &c.
subs: (-meṅ), To apply perfume or ʻit̤r to the body or clothes
(-par), To rub perfume (on), &c. ( = ʻit̤r lagānā, q.v.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عطر ('itr), from Arabic عِطْر (ʕiṭr).

ety: S. अति+क्रमणं
def: v.n. To exult greatly or unduly (by reason of, -par, wealth, success, &c.); to behave with pride, or self-conceitedness, or boastfulness, or arrogance, or insolence; to give oneself airs (by reason of), to be conceited or vain or supercilious; to act affectedly or coquettishly; to strut, swagger, show off.
adj. (f. اترائي itrāʼī), Conceited, vain, consequential, supercilious, proud, haughty, arrogant; strutting; affected; playful, wanton, coquettish.
s.m. = اتري پهل itrī-phal, q.v.

ety: S. त्रि+फला
def: s.m. An electuary composed of three sorts of myrobalans with coriander seed and honey (commonly ترپهلا tirphalā).
s.m. = اتري پهل itrī-phal, q.v.
s.f. pl. (of next, q.v.), Perfumes, &c.

ety: for A. عطرية ʻit̤r+aff. īyat
def: s.f. Fragrance, perfume, odour, scent.
